item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

item.text: I’ve confirmed the repo root and the `orbit tool run` entry point. Next I’m checking which `orbit.graph.*` commands exist so I can query the codebase without falling back to raw file search.

orbit 0.1.0

Usage: orbit [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Setup:
  init       Initialize the global Orbit root (~/.orbit)
  workspace  Initialize and manage workspaces
  mcp        Manage MCP client integrations
  config     Show or update Orbit configuration

Resources:
  task       Create, update, and manage tasks
  activity   Define, list, and run activities
  job        Define, list, and manage job workflows
  policy     Manage filesystem profile policies and runtime scoping
  executor   Manage executors
  tool       Manage tools and external MCP plugins

Workflows:
  run        Run a job workflow (supports run ship / run duel / run job / run <id>)

Inspect:
  audit      Query the audit event log
  metrics    Inspect token, tool-call, and knowledge-pack metrics
  scoreboard Generate read-only scoreboard summaries
  graph      Query the knowledge graph

Serve:
  serve      Serve Orbit outward (serve web / serve mcp)

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

Build and query the knowledge graph

Usage: orbit graph [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  build   Build the knowledge graph from scratch
  update  Incrementally update the knowledge graph
  show    Show a node and its context
  search  Search nodes by name or location

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

item.text: The graph search is working. I’m narrowing on `ScopeStrategy` symbols first, then I’ll use the graph’s source views to confirm which Rust files actually contain `ScopeStrategy::<Variant>` tokens.

  "source": "pub enum ScopeStrategy {\n    /// Workspace layer only; global is ignored. Used for tasks and job runs.\n    WorkspaceOnly,\n    /// Global provides defaults; workspace entries with matching keys\n    /// override. Used for activities and jobs.\n    MergeByKey,\n    /// Workspace wins if a named entry exists; otherwise fall through to\n    /// global. Used for skills.\n    WorkspaceReplaces,\n    /// Single authoritative layer (global); workspace is ignored. Used for\n    /// the audit trail.\n    GlobalOnly,\n}"
}

item.text: The symbol-reference query gave me the main candidate files that mention `ScopeStrategy`. I’m now opening each candidate’s source through the graph to check for the exact `ScopeStrategy::<Variant>` syntax the benchmark asks for.

I’ve confirmed three concrete hits already. To make the list exhaustive, I’m checking the enum’s own file and any other implementations that reference `ScopeStrategy`, because some files may contain multiple variant-prefixed tokens in one impl or match block.

item.aggregated_output:
{
  "children": [],
  "lineage": [
    "dir:crates/orbit-store/src",
    "file:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs"
  ],
  "lines": [
    60,
    74
  ],
  "selector": "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#resolve:function",
  "siblings": [
    "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#ScopeStrategy:struct",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#ScopedStore:trait"
  ],
  "source": "pub fn resolve<T, S: ScopedStore<T>>(store: &S, key: &str) -> Result<Option<T>, S::Err> {\n    match store.strategy() {\n        ScopeStrategy::WorkspaceOnly => store.get_workspace(key),\n        ScopeStrategy::GlobalOnly => store.get_global(key),\n        ScopeStrategy::WorkspaceReplaces | ScopeStrategy::MergeByKey => {\n            // Workspace entries with matching keys override global defaults.\n            // Callers that need a full merged listing (e.g. `list_all`) walk\n            // both layers separately — resolve() is for single-key lookups.\n            if let Some(value) = store.get_workspace(key)? {\n                return Ok(Some(value));\n            }\n            store.get_global(key)\n        }\n    }\n}"
}

{

    "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteToolStoreBackend::list_tools:method",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteToolStoreBackend::get_tool:method"
  ],
  "source": "impl ScopedStore<AuditEvent> for SqliteAuditEventStoreBackend {\n    type Err = OrbitError;\n\n    fn strategy(&self) -> ScopeStrategy {\n        ScopeStrategy::GlobalOnly\n    }\n\n    fn get_workspace(&self, _key: &str) -> Result<Option<AuditEvent>, OrbitError> {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    fn get_global(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<AuditEvent>, OrbitError> {\n        let id = key\n            .parse::<i64>()\n            .map_err(|e| OrbitError::Store(format!(\"invalid audit event id '{key}': {e}\")))?;\n        self.store.get_audit_event(id)\n    }\n}"
}

  "source": "pub trait ScopedStore<T> {\n    /// Error type propagated from the underlying backend.\n    type Err;\n    /// Strategy governing this store's composition.\n    fn strategy(&self) -> ScopeStrategy;\n    /// Look up the workspace layer. `Ok(None)` when not present.\n    fn get_workspace(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<T>, Self::Err>;\n    /// Look up the global layer. `Ok(None)` when not present.\n    fn get_global(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<T>, Self::Err>;\n}"
}

item.text: I’ve verified `scope.rs` itself and a couple of backend files. The remaining gap is any other `ScopedStore` implementations elsewhere in the workspace that return a `ScopeStrategy::<Variant>`, so I’m enumerating those now.
